rationale_top3: Entertainment Excellence (19/20): Exceptional range of activities including heated indoor pool with two spas and huge TV screen, jumping pillow, pedal go-karts, games room with PlayStations, basketball and tennis courts, gym, and during school holidays daily kids club activities and movie nights with free popcorn. Families consistently note 'kids never want to leave' and 'so much to do we didn't need to leave the park.' Revolutionary Pet-Friendly Infrastructure (16/20 Nature): Features fully fenced individual caravan sites and cabins where dogs can roam freely, plus a stunning 'BigPaw Billabong' off-leash area with natural ponds, rocks, waterfalls and swimming areas exclusively for dogs (one family at a time). Multiple reviews cite this as 'best pet friendly park we have ever stayed at.' Spotless Cleanliness (14/15): Overwhelming praise for 'immaculate' facilities with 'cleanest bathrooms I've ever encountered at a holiday park.' Newly renovated heated amenities blocks are consistently highlighted, with thoughtful touches like radio playing in bathrooms and staff who walk guests to their sites.
rationale_honourable: Prime Location (10/10): Positioned on western edge of Ballarat about 5km from CBD attractions like Sovereign Hill, with Woolworths and shopping centre within 1km walking distance. Adjacent to Mullawallah Wetlands with boardwalks and birdlife, providing convenient access to both town amenities and natural settings. Outstanding Value Perception (12/15): Despite pricing ranging from $45-$116 per night, most families find good value for the comprehensive facilities offered, with many noting the cost is justified by the exceptional entertainment options and pet infrastructure.
water_fun: Heated indoor pool, two spas, splash pad
kids_play: Jumping pillow, playgrounds, pedal karts, games room
pet_detail: Fully fenced individual pet sites with synthetic turf, pet-friendly cabins with enclosed yards, dedicated off-leash 'BigPaw Billabong' area with ponds and waterfalls (one family at a time), dog wash facilities, dogs not permitted on furniture or beds in cabins
best_for: This park is ideal for families who want comprehensive on-site entertainment and activities without needing to leave the park, and especially outstanding for families travelling with dogs who want secure, spacious facilities
executive_summary:
PARK: BIG4 Ballarat Windmill Holiday Park
SCORE: 89/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-18
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 418

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (19/20) — Reviews consistently praise the exceptional range of activities including heated indoor pool with two spas and huge TV screen, jumping pillow, pedal go-karts, games room with PlayStations, basketball and tennis courts, gym, and during school holidays daily kids club activities and movie nights with free popcorn. Multiple families noted "kids never want to leave" and "so much to do we didn't need to leave the park."

Nature (16/20) — The park features "big trees that make it feel like a forest sanctuary," beautifully maintained gardens with roses, park chickens to feed, and direct access to adjacent Mullawallah Wetlands with boardwalks and abundant birdlife. The standout natural feature is the landscaped 'BigPaw Billabong' dog park with natural ponds, waterfalls, and rock formations, though the park itself is not in a particularly scenic bush or coastal setting.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Overwhelming praise for cleanliness with reviews describing "spotlessly clean," "immaculate," and "cleanest bathrooms I've ever encountered at a holiday park." The newly renovated heated amenities blocks are consistently highlighted, though a few older reviews mentioned occasional maintenance issues in some older cabins and minor cleaning oversights.

Value (12/15) — Pricing ranges from $45-$116 per night depending on season and site type, with most families finding it good value for the facilities offered. Some reviews noted it's "not cheap" with dynamic pricing, and pet-friendly sites cost extra ($65 mentioned), but many felt the comprehensive facilities justified the cost. A few reviews criticized pricing as expensive compared to other parks.

Site Size (8/10) — Reviews indicate sites are "good size," "spacious," and "plenty of room for large van and car," with easy drive-through access and wide sealed roads. The fully fenced pet sites are described as very large. However, some reviews noted regular sites are "very close to each other" and a few mentioned tight, not-quite-level spots.

Sentiment (10/10) — Exceptional sentiment with numerous "best park we've ever stayed at" comments, high repeat visitation ("been back five times," "4th time staying"), enthusiastic recommendations, and families specifically rebooking before departure. Staff consistently praised as "amazing," "friendly," and going "above and beyond." Very few negative reviews among 418 spanning three years.

Location (10/10) — Located on western edge of Ballarat, about 5km from CBD attractions like Sovereign Hill, with Woolworths and shopping centre within 1km walking distance. Easy access via main road, positioned between town conveniences and peaceful setting. Adjacent to wetlands reserve and close to rail trail. Reviews consistently note the convenient yet quiet location.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Site surfaces are inconsistent - many powered sites are gravel or dirt rather than grass, with some reviews complaining about dust, mud in wet weather, and "no grass at all" despite being advertised as grass sites.

2. Amenities capacity struggles during peak periods - multiple reviews from school holidays mention overcrowding, limited shower availability, one amenities block serving the whole park at times, and the pool/spa being constantly crowded.

3. Value perception mixed with some guests finding pricing too high - $95-116 per night for powered sites struck some as expensive, pet sites require significant surcharge, and dynamic pricing can catch families off-guard, with a few reviews citing "greed" and feeling overcharged for what was delivered.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"best pet friendly park we have ever stayed at," "heated indoor pool with 2 spas and huge TV," "fully fenced dog sites," "spotlessly clean amenities," "kids never want to leave," "friendly and helpful staff going above and beyond," "dog billabong is awesome," "cleanest bathrooms I've ever encountered at a holiday park"

rationale_top3: Entertainment (16/20): Extensive family facilities including huge jumping pillow, indoor heated pool and spa, outdoor pool, games room with PlayStations and pool table, pedal carts/go-karts, indoor and outdoor playgrounds, tennis and basketball courts, plus organized daily activities during peak periods. Nature (12/20): Urban holiday park setting with leafy green shaded trees and a lake across the road. Limited natural features with no beach or bushwalks. Primarily a heritage tourism location in Ballarat's Victorian goldfields area. Cleanliness (11/15): Mixed reviews with many praising spotless amenities and grounds, but significant negative feedback about cabin cleanliness including floors not vacuumed/mopped, sticky surfaces, dust, stained bedding, and black mould in showers. Inconsistent standards between sites and accommodation types.
rationale_honourable: NRMA Ballarat Holiday Park earns Silver recognition for its excellent family entertainment facilities and prime location near Sovereign Hill. The park consistently impresses families with its jumping pillow, indoor heated pool, spa, games room with PlayStations, pedal carts, and both indoor and outdoor playgrounds. While cleanliness issues appear in some reviews (particularly floors and cabin maintenance), the park's comprehensive amenities, friendly staff, and walkable distance to Ballarat's key attractions make it a solid choice for families visiting the region.
water_fun: Indoor heated pool and spa plus outdoor pool
kids_play: Jumping pillow, pedal carts, indoor/outdoor playgrounds, games room
pet_detail: Pet friendly (dogs allowed), phone ahead to confirm site availability
best_for: This park is best for families with children aged 3-12 who want extensive on-site activities and easy walking access to Sovereign Hill.
executive_summary:
PARK: NRMA Ballarat Holiday Park
SCORE: 72/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-21
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 160

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (16/20) — Reviews consistently praise the extensive kids' facilities including a huge jumping pillow, indoor heated pool and spa, outdoor pool, games room with PlayStations and pool table, pedal carts/go-karts, indoor and outdoor playgrounds, tennis and basketball courts, plus organized daily activities during peak periods. Some equipment occasionally out of service (chess set, some carts) and the games room can be expensive ($50 per day mentioned). Indoor play area noted as best for toddlers with less for older kids.

Nature (12/20) — Park described as having "leafy green shady trees" and a lake mentioned across the road. Primarily an urban holiday park setting in Ballarat with no beach, bush walks, or significant natural features. The natural environment is limited to basic landscaping and proximity to Victorian goldfields heritage area. Not a nature-focused destination.

Cleanliness (11/15) — Highly mixed reviews with many praising "spotless" and "incredibly clean" amenities and grounds, but significant negative feedback about cabin cleanliness. Multiple reviews cite floors not vacuumed/mopped, sticky surfaces, dust, dirty couches, stained bedding, black mould in showers, and ant problems. Some mention toilet blocks running out of supplies during peak times. Camp kitchen occasionally messy. Cleanliness appears inconsistent between sites and accommodation types.

Value (11/15) — Reviews frequently mention the park is "pricey" ($53-57+ per night for powered sites, $300+ per night for cabins). Minimum stay requirements (2-3 nights) frustrate some guests. Many feel "well worth it" for the facilities and location, particularly families using all amenities. Dynamic pricing criticized with park "30% empty" but charging peak rates. Good facilities but premium pricing for the Ballarat region.

Site Size (7/10) — Sites described as "large" and "good size" by many, but significant complaints about sites being "very small," "tight," and "near impossible for larger vans." Concrete slabs available but positioning issues with tow hitches ending up on roads. Staff criticized for not matching van size to appropriate sites. Some guests damaged vehicles maneuvering. Ensuite sites well-reviewed. Inconsistent site sizing and allocation.

Sentiment (8/10) — Strong repeat visitation with guests booking "same spot for same time next year" and families returning for annual trips. Staff consistently praised as "friendly," "helpful," and "accommodating." Many "will definitely be back" comments from families. However, cleanliness issues and pricing complaints create hesitation, with some stating "won't be back" or left early. Generally positive for families using full facilities, mixed for those experiencing cleanliness or maintenance problems.

Location (7/10) — Excellent proximity to Sovereign Hill (walking distance, 5-10 minutes), close to Ballarat CBD, restaurants, and pubs within walking distance. Convenient for exploring Ballarat attractions. However, it's an urban location without beach or natural setting. Entrance road has potholes. Not a scenic destination itself but perfectly positioned for Ballarat tourism.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Inconsistent cabin and accommodation cleanliness with multiple reports of dirty floors, stained bedding, dusty surfaces, mould in bathrooms, and inadequate cleaning between guests, significantly impacting guest experience especially given premium pricing.
2. Site sizing and allocation problems causing difficulties for larger caravans and motorhomes, with poor staff awareness leading to guest frustration, vehicle damage, and inability to use sites as booked.
3. Premium pricing with dynamic rates and mandatory minimum stays that many guests find excessive for the regional location, particularly when facilities are occasionally closed or cleanliness standards aren't met, reducing perceived value for money.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"lots to do for kids", "very close to Sovereign Hill", "jumping pillow", "indoor heated pool/spa", "floors not vacuumed or mopped", "cleanliness really needs to improve", "extremely clean and tidy", "pricey", "friendly staff", "daily activities for the kids", "sites very small", "would highly recommend"

rationale_top3: Entertainment (13/20): Free access to the adjacent Eureka Swimming Pool is the standout family feature, though onsite facilities like the games room have broken equipment, mini golf needs maintenance, and the playground has been removed, limiting options for young children. Nature (14/20): Beautiful big shady trees throughout the park provide natural ambience and a pleasant country feel, with proximity to Eureka Stockade museum, walking trails, Lake Wendouree and Botanical Gardens enhancing the nature experience despite the urban setting. Cleanliness (12/15): Generally clean cabins and facilities with many reviews praising immaculate amenities, though inconsistent standards noted with some reports of dirty toilets, unserviced ensuite sites during extended stays, and aging cabin infrastructure.
rationale_honourable: Eureka Stockade Holiday Park offers families a solid base in Ballarat with clean cabins, friendly staff, and the standout bonus of free access to the adjacent aquatic centre. While the park has some aging facilities and limited onsite entertainment beyond a games room and mini golf that needs maintenance, it delivers good value for families seeking comfortable accommodation close to Sovereign Hill and other Ballarat attractions without the premium pricing of larger chains.
water_fun: Free access to adjacent aquatic centre
kids_play: Games room, mini golf, basketball court available
best_for: This park best suits families with young children (under 10) who prioritize clean, comfortable accommodation and pool access over extensive onsite play facilities, and who plan to spend days exploring Ballarat's attractions like Sovereign Hill.
executive_summary:
PARK: Eureka Stockade Holiday Park
SCORE: 71/100
DATE SCORED: 2024-01-15
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 86

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (13/20) — Free access to adjacent Eureka Swimming Pool is the standout feature mentioned repeatedly by families. Onsite entertainment includes a games room (though multiple reviews note broken/out-of-order equipment), mini golf needing maintenance, and a new basketball court. Critical concern: playground was removed, disappointing families with young children. Limited structured activities compared to larger chains.

Nature (14/20) — Park features "beautiful big shady trees" providing natural ambience and shade throughout the site. Located near Eureka Stockade museum with walking trails nearby. Families appreciate the "nice country feel" despite urban location. However, it's primarily an urban park rather than bushland/nature setting, with proximity to Lake Wendouree and Botanical Gardens requiring short drives.

Cleanliness (12/15) — Strong mixed feedback on cleanliness. Majority of recent reviews praise "clean cabins," "immaculately clean" facilities, and "clean amenities." However, concerning negative reviews cite "dirty toilets," "broken toilet seats," "paper on floors," and ensuite sites not serviced over 7-night stays. Cabins generally well-maintained but some described as "tired and rundown" or "old and rough."

Value (11/15) — Reviews show divided opinions on value. Positive mentions of "good value for money," G'day member discounts, and budget-friendly options. Critical reviews cite "expensive for a tiny patch of uneven black gravel" at $70/night, "not worth $100+ per night" for basic cabins, and disappointment with site quality versus pricing. Free pool access adds significant value for families.

Site Size (6/10) — Multiple complaints about site size, particularly powered sites. Reviews mention "tiny patch," "didn't have space for caravan awning," "not enough room to bring awning out," and "gravel site which didn't have space." Some cabins described as "small" with dining areas "too small to cater for five people." Drive-through sites mentioned positively but space remains a concern.

Sentiment (8/10) — Overall positive sentiment from families, with many "would definitely stay again" comments and repeat visitors. Staff consistently praised as "friendly," "helpful," and "accommodating." However, notable negative reviews state "won't be back," "2nd stay but will be our last," and "couldn't recommend." Strong loyalty from satisfied guests but maintenance issues frustrate others.

Location (7/10) — Excellent proximity to Ballarat attractions: "5min walk to Eureka stockade museum," "minutes away from Sovereign Hill," "close to town." Supermarket 600m away, IGA walking distance. "Small drive into Ballarat City" appreciated. Bus stop at entrance. Some noise concerns from adjacent facilities with "loud megaphone announcements" and occasional "rowdy locals" with late-night disturbances.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Limited and poorly maintained onsite entertainment facilities with broken games room equipment, overgrown mini golf, and removed playground disappointing families expecting play areas for young children.
2. Inconsistent cleanliness standards with multiple reports of dirty toilet facilities, unserviced ensuite sites over extended stays, and aging cabin infrastructure despite many positive cleanliness reviews.
3. Site size and space issues significantly impacting caravan stays, with numerous complaints about inadequate room for awnings, small gravel sites, and cramped powered sites that don't accommodate modern caravans comfortably.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"free access to pool next door", "Games Room is full of broken, out-of-order games", "playground had been removed", "tiny patch of uneven black gravel", "Beautiful big shady trees", "staff were absolutely amazing and kind", "didn't have space for the caravan awning", "clean, well maintained", "tired and rundown", "perfect for a family weekend away", "toilets were dirty", "close to everything"

rationale_top3: Entertainment facilities are extremely dated and unsuitable for young children, with a 1970s games room described as frightening by a 9-year-old, no camp kitchen, and minimal activities - clearly positioning this as a stopover rather than a family holiday destination. Persistent highway noise is a serious issue with multiple families reporting inability to sleep, noise penetrating through camper trailers, and significantly impacting the rest and relaxation essential for family holidays. Maintenance concerns including confirmed black mold in cabin corners and mattresses, rust and mold in bathrooms, and general run-down appearance despite cleanliness efforts raise health and safety concerns for families with children.
rationale_honourable: Strong value proposition at $33-36 per night compared to $45+ elsewhere, with consistently clean amenities and spacious sites making it a functional budget stopover for exploring Ballarat attractions.
water_fun: Pool available but not heated for swimming
kids_play: Basic playground, very dated 1970s games room
pet_detail: Pet friendly with large grassed areas for dogs to run and exercise.
best_for: Families with older children who need an affordable, clean base for exploring Ballarat attractions rather than seeking on-site entertainment and activities.
executive_summary:
PARK: Shady Acres Caravan Park
SCORE: 48/100
DATE SCORED: 2024-01-22
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 49

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (6/20) — Multiple reviews explicitly state the park is "not ideal for young children." The games room is described as "dated from the 1970s" with "flies" and one child felt "someone had died in there." While there's a pool and playground mentioned, entertainment infrastructure is minimal with no camp kitchen and paid BBQs (20c per 10 minutes). This is clearly a stopover park rather than a destination for families seeking activities.

Nature (10/20) — Reviews mention "acres of grassed area," "lovely lawned sites," "lots of room to run," and "bird life prolific." However, the park is described as close to the highway with significant traffic noise. There's no nearby beach, creek, or bushland mentioned. One review notes "valley with bushes and rabbit poo everywhere." The natural setting is basic suburban grassland without standout natural features.

Cleanliness (10/15) — This is the park's strongest attribute with consistent praise: "very clean amenities," "spotlessly clean," "amenities super clean and fresh," "very very clean." However, several reviews mention concerns including "BLACK MOLD" in cabin corners and mattresses, "rust visible, mould on windows," "basin drain holes needs basic clean," and general "run down" appearance. Recent reviews emphasize cleanliness despite aged facilities.

Value (13/15) — Strong value proposition with consistent mentions of "$33-36 per night" compared to "$45+ elsewhere," "great price," "very reasonable price," and "excellent prices." Multiple reviews note "you get what you pay for" and call it "cheap and decent" for basic stopover needs. Families appreciate the affordability for exploring Ballarat attractions on a budget.

Site Size (8/10) — Reviews describe "large spacious camping area," "plenty of space," "large sites," "good sized" caravan sites, and "very roomy." Sites are mostly gravel/road base with adequate room for rigs. Some mention "dusty with very little grass" and exposure to wind depending on site location.

Sentiment (7/10) — Mixed sentiment with many positive comments about friendly staff and accommodation, but significant concerns about suitability for families. Several "would stay again" mentions from satisfied guests, but multiple 1-2 star reviews from disappointed families. High permanent resident population noted. Overall sentiment is "good for overnight" but "not a holiday destination."

Location (7/10) — Described as "10 minutes from town," "5 minutes to town," "just off the highway easy access," and "short drive into central Ballarat." Convenient for Kryal Castle (5 min away) and Ballarat attractions. However, significant highway noise is a major complaint with one family unable to sleep. Location is functional rather than scenic.

WHY NOT 100:
1. The park openly acknowledges it's "not ideal for young children" with extremely dated entertainment facilities including a 1970s games room that frightened a 9-year-old, no camp kitchen, and minimal kids' activities—making it unsuitable as a family holiday destination.
2. Highway noise is a recurring serious complaint with multiple reviews mentioning "horrible" traffic noise that prevented sleep, "very noisy," and noise going "straight through the camper trailers canvas"—significantly impacting family rest and relaxation.
3. Maintenance issues are concerning for families including confirmed black mold in cabin corners and mattresses, rust and mold in bathrooms, "neglected" cabin repairs, inconsistent hot water, and overall "run down" appearance despite cleanliness efforts—raising health and comfort concerns.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Not ideal for young children", "games room was a little dated (from the 1970s)", "my 9 year old said she thinks someone had died in there", "highway noise was horrible none of my family slept", "BLACK MOLD in the corners", "step back in time for a basic camp", "good for a stop over", "not a holiday destination by any stretch", "amenities are old but very clean", "$34 a night its great value", "Pet friendly", "lots of room to run your fur baby"

park_name: BIG4 Kryal Castle Holiday Park - Ballarat
rationale_top3: Severe and consistent cleanliness failures across amenities, accommodations, and facilities, with multiple reports of unsanitary bathrooms, uncleaned rooms, and health hazards like black mold and blood-stained sheets that are completely unacceptable for a Big4 standard park. The park appears to have operated while still under construction with building materials, safety hazards, "out of order" signs on most facilities, inadequate camp kitchen ("one cook top, ONE single sink for 100 people"), no playground, no pool, creating a fundamentally incomplete holiday park experience. Systemic operational failures including last-minute booking cancellations (some just 6 days before arrival), poor communication, inadequate staff training, inconsistent facility availability, and management that appears unresponsive to serious complaints, severely damaging trust and reliability.
rationale_honourable: Entertainment score of 12/20 driven entirely by adjacent Kryal Castle access with medieval shows, jousting, animals, face painting, and potion classes that guests consistently praised, though the caravan park itself offers no onsite entertainment facilities.
water_fun: No pool or spa facilities available.
kids_play: No playground; castle access provides entertainment.
best_for: Families who prioritize access to Kryal Castle medieval entertainment over traditional holiday park amenities and facilities.
executive_summary:
PARK: BIG4 Kryal Castle Holiday Park - Ballarat
SCORE: 42/100
DATE SCORED: 2025
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 76

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (12/20) — Reviews consistently praised Kryal Castle access with "great shows & entertainment", "jousting is fun", "kids loved the animals", face painting, potion classes, and medieval-themed activities. However, the caravan park itself has "no playground", "no pool or spas", "nothing for kids to do" outside castle hours, and "no play equipment or spaces for kids to play".

Nature (10/20) — Guests appreciated "great views overlooking Ballarat", "awesome view", and "soft grass" at some sites. Location offers countryside setting with horses in paddocks. However, many reviews describe the site as feeling like "a building site" with "construction stuff everywhere, rubbles, rusty iron stuff", detracting significantly from natural enjoyment.

Cleanliness (3/15) — This is the park's most critical weakness. Multiple recent reviews report "toilets and showers were disgusting - poo on floor, toilet seats half hanging off", "no cleaning or emptying", "blood stained sheets", "hair on towels and bedsheets", "black mold over bathroom ceiling", "carpets are stained and dirty with cobwebs", "floor was greasy and sticky", and "not once across 4 nights were amenities cleaned".

Value (5/15) — Highly mixed sentiment. Positive reviews noted "great value inclusive of entry into Kryal Castle", "very reasonably priced", and "extremely good value for money with entry and breakfast included". However, many felt "completely ripped off", cited "$72 per night" as poor value given limited facilities, "$300 a night" for tiny homes with issues, and "this is the WORST experience of all" for a Big4 park.

Site Size (4/10) — Significant negative feedback: "sites are small and poor designed", "had to take up 2 sites", "no room to open our awning", "cabin was extremely tiny", "rooms themselves are cramped", and "family room was very VERY small". Positive mentions included "sites are spacious" and "site was plenty big enough for a 10p tent" from some tent campers.

Sentiment (3/10) — Overwhelmingly negative overall sentiment with many one-star reviews. Common phrases: "worst Big4 experience ever", "DO NOT book your holiday here", "we won't be going back", "lasted literally 2 hours and left", "we lasted 2 nights out of 3 booked", "would visit again but wouldn't stay there". Major issues with last-minute booking cancellations affecting multiple families. Positive reviews mainly from 2024-2026 focusing on castle experience.

Location (5/10) — Reviews praised "great location overlooking Ballarat", "easy access from freeway", and "easy to get to all sites around Ballarat". Castle adjacency is the main draw. However, "reception is actually IN Kryal Castle, which took a while to find", poor signage, "no map of caravan park", and confusion about facilities detracted from location benefits.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Severe and consistent cleanliness failures across amenities, accommodations, and facilities, with multiple reports of unsanitary bathrooms, uncleaned rooms, and health hazards like black mold and blood-stained sheets that are completely unacceptable for a Big4 standard park.

2. The park appears to have operated while still under construction with building materials, safety hazards, "out of order" signs on most facilities, inadequate camp kitchen ("one cook top, ONE single sink for 100 people"), no playground, no pool, creating a fundamentally incomplete holiday park experience.

3. Systemic operational failures including last-minute booking cancellations (some just 6 days before arrival), poor communication, inadequate staff training, inconsistent facility availability, and management that appears unresponsive to serious complaints, severely damaging trust and reliability.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"toilets and showers were disgusting - poo on floor, toilet seats half hanging off", "worst Big4 experience ever", "it's not ready", "building materials all over the grounds", "nothing for kids to do. NOTHING", "castle entertainment and attractions were great", "sites are small and poor designed", "we lasted literally 2 hours in there and left"
